word: G5516 - χξϛ - chx stigma - khee xee stig'-ma - Six hundred threescore and six

sections.paragraph:
- Original: χξς

- Transliteration: Chi xi stigma

- Phonetic: khee xee stig'-ma

- Definition:

1. six hundred and sixty six, the meaning of which is the basis of much vain speculation

- Origin: the G22d, G14th and an obsolete letter (G4742 as a cross) of the Greek alphabet (intermediate between the G5th and G6th), used as numbers

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech:

- Strong's: the 22nd 14th and an obsolete letter (G4742 as a cross) of the Greek alphabet (intermediate between the 5th and 6th) used as numbers; denoting respectively 600 60 and 6; 666 as a numeral: - six hundred threescore and six.
